caption:
This is the ONLY type of person who should be chasing prestige

#roylee #cluely #motivationallines #ivyleague
transcript: This is going to be one of my least viral reels and I will try to up the verbal complexity so that it only really hits the target audience here. I actually, for all the talk that I have about prestige, I don't principally disagree with the concept of chasing prestige or going after it. What I principally disagree with is the concept of exerting too much effort to achieve prestige that is not going to be worth the effort that you put in. The thing is for some people, they're just overwhelmingly competent that something like getting into Harvard or Stanford is pretty trivial for them. And if you're watching this video, the odds are actually quite high that you're one of these people, but you know, getting a 1560 plus on the SAT really wasn't that big of a deal. And if it's going to be rather natural and easy for you to get into a top school, then you should. Because there are actual real material advantages to prestige. If you go to a top school, you're going to get access to investor interest much quicker. The alumni network is actually real and there's a reason why everyone in Silicon Valley walks around the Stanford hoodie and everyone on Wall Street walks around the little Princeton Jersey. The problem is that most people are normies and they're never going to be able to achieve anything that is even remotely prestigious. So for me, the advice that I ought to give to normies is the advice of just blanket do not chase prestige, whereas for actually very competent people, you know, and you know who you are, just do the things that have the best risk reward matrix. If getting into Harvard is easy, then go to Harvard, even for a day.
